title: Outdoor Bar Stools
summary: Simple slatted bar stools to match the Simple Outdoor Collection bar table — a quick, sturdy 1x2 and 1x3 build you can knock out several of.
description:
These are the companion stools for a patio bar table: a slatted seat with breadboard ends on four straight legs, all from 1x2 and 1x3 stock. They're deliberately plain and repeatable, so you can build a matching set in an afternoon, and everything is glue plus screws — no angles, no joinery to fuss over.

Build the seat frame first and make sure it's square, because the breadboard ends and slats all reference off it. A handy trick from the plan: when you attach the breadboard ends, set a seat slat in between them as a spacer to get the gap right without measuring. The seat slats space evenly so rain drains through — important for anything living outside.

Since these are outdoor pieces, use exterior-rated screws and glue and finish them properly — at minimum a base coat, touching up screw holes with paintable silicone or filler, a light sand, and a topcoat. Build them tall to suit a bar-height table (a counter-height table wants shorter legs), and check they sit level and don't rock before you call one done.
timeLabel: 3–5 hrs for a stool, less for a set

tools.note: Optional; the stool goes together with glue and screws.

materials.name: 1x2 board, 8 ft
materials.note: Aprons, supports, bottom supports. ~12 ft total.

materials.name: 1x3 board, 8 ft
materials.note: Legs, slats, breadboard ends. ~18 ft total.

steps.title: Build the seat frame
steps.body: Build the seat frame from the side and end aprons and the two supports with glue and 2" screws. Make sure it's square — the slats and breadboard ends all reference off it.

steps.title: Attach the breadboard ends
steps.body: Attach the 1x3 breadboard ends with 1-1/4" screws and glue. Set a seat slat between them as a spacer to line them up without measuring.

steps.title: Add the seat slats
steps.body: Screw the five seat slats to the supports with glue and 1-1/4" screws, spaced evenly so rain drains through.

steps.title: Attach the legs
steps.body: Attach the four legs to the seat aprons with 1-1/4" screws and glue. Check the stool sits level and doesn't rock.

steps.title: Add bottom supports and finish
steps.body: Attach the longer bottom supports to both the shorter supports and the legs with 1-1/4" screws and glue, tying the legs together low. Give the stool at least a base finish coat, touch up screw holes with paintable silicone or filler, sand lightly, and add a topcoat.
